title: Olearia rudis
intro: Olearia rudis, also known as rough tree daisy or rough woolly daisy, is a shrub native to New South Wales, Australia, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Olearia rudis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Olearia rudis is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or topical cream, and is sometimes used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and minor injuries.
taxonomy: Olearia rudis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants. Olearia rudis is further classified within the genus Olearia, which is a group of shrubs and small trees native to Australia and New Zealand. The genus Olearia is part of the tribe Astereae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Olearia rudis is also known by its common name, rough tree daisy, due to its unique appearance and growth habits.
